meta.coverage: Includes statement walkthrough content and Q&A operational rules

extraction_method.pass_1: Enumerated all candidate analysis points from the full transcript, including trivial checks and examples.
extraction_method.pass_2: Normalized processor examples into universal rules, preserved thresholds/formulas, and deduplicated overlapping logic with rationale.

elements.name: Two-bucket cost model
elements.description: Merchant processing costs must be split into card-brand costs and processor costs before any optimization analysis.
elements.rule_or_threshold: Card-brand fees + processor fees = total cost model baseline
recommended_action: Separate fee lines into card-brand vs processor-owned buckets before benchmarking.
source_trace: S02: 'there's really kind of two buckets where your fees come from'

elements.name: Interchange table breadth awareness
elements.description: Analysis logic must account for many interchange outcomes rather than assuming a single flat wholesale card rate.
elements.rule_or_threshold: ~400 interchange rates exist
recommended_action: Model interchange as multi-rate, not flat-rate.
source_trace: S02: 'there are 400 different rates ... called interchange rates'

elements.name: Card-brand fee share baseline
elements.description: Card-brand/interchange component usually represents most of total program cost.
elements.rule_or_threshold: Card-brand share typically 60%-80%
recommended_action: Compare observed share against 60%-80% baseline to detect classification or markup anomalies.
source_trace: S02: 'that's going to make between 60 and 80% of what you're paying'

elements.name: Processor fee share baseline
elements.description: Processor-owned markup and ancillary fees are expected minority share but still optimization target.
elements.rule_or_threshold: Processor share typically 20%-40%
recommended_action: Flag processor share materially above expected range for pricing review.
source_trace: S02: 'payment processor fees ... 20 to 40% of your program'

elements.name: No special wholesale myth check
elements.description: Do not accept claims of exclusive wholesale card-brand pricing from processors.
elements.rule_or_threshold: All processors pay same card-brand wholesale fees
elements.trigger_condition: When proposal claims unique wholesale access
recommended_action: Validate comparisons based on processor markup and service terms, not wholesale claims.
source_trace: S02: 'all credit card processor pay the exact same amount in those card brand fees'

elements.name: Net effective rate as primary KPI
elements.description: Overall statement health should be measured first by net effective rate.
elements.rule_or_threshold: Net effective rate is the core decision KPI
recommended_action: Display net effective rate in every analysis output.
source_trace: S02: 'fees divided by your sales your net effective rate ... the one rate that matters'

elements.name: Interchange drivers
elements.description: Interchange variation depends on where/how card is used and whether card is present.
recommended_action: Include card-present/card-not-present context in all fee variance explanations.
source_trace: S02: 'depend on where you use the card ... was card present or card not present'

elements.name: Representative month integrity check
elements.description: Savings analysis must use a representative month and avoid cherry-picked high-volume periods.
elements.rule_or_threshold: Reject non-representative outlier month inputs
recommended_action: Validate month against trailing trend before quoting expected savings.
source_trace: S03: 'make sure they're not taking a very busy month and overpromise savings'

elements.name: Monthly stability check
elements.description: Use month-to-month trend stability to assess whether period is suitable for benchmark comparisons.
recommended_action: If unstable seasonality exists, run multi-month weighted analysis.
source_trace: S03: statement graph used to confirm 'pretty equal value most months'

elements.name: Statement sectioning baseline
elements.description: Analysis should parse statement into summary, interchange detail, processor markup, and add-on fees.
recommended_action: Use deterministic section tags to map each fee line.
source_trace: S03-S09 walkthrough repeatedly follows summary -> interchange -> markup -> ancillary fees

elements.name: Interchange detail row capture
elements.description: For each interchange row capture transaction count, volume, rate, per-item fee, and total paid.
recommended_action: Store row-level detail for audit and downgrade detection.
source_trace: S04: 'number of transactions ... amount ... rate ... per item fee ... what you paid'

elements.name: Basis-point notation normalization
elements.description: Statements may express basis points in different numeric formats; normalize before comparisons.
elements.rule_or_threshold: Example mapping: 1.82% = 182 bps; 0.80% may appear as 0.008
recommended_action: Normalize all rates to basis points and percent in output.
source_trace: S04/S07: examples of 1.82, 1.38 and 0.008 display styles

elements.name: Blended-line parsing
elements.description: Some processors combine interchange and markup in one line while still exposing underlying components.
elements.trigger_condition: When statement bundles top and bottom values per card type
recommended_action: Split blended rows into card-brand and processor components before analysis.
source_trace: S06: 'top number is markup ... bottom number is interchange'

elements.name: Fine-print change scan
elements.description: Fine print often contains fee increases and acceptance language tied to continued account use.
recommended_action: Diff all legal/notice blocks month-over-month and surface changes prominently.
source_trace: S07: 'buried ... effective Sept ... if you use your account you accept these terms'

elements.name: Online-only notice risk
elements.description: Notices directing merchants to website-only fee changes increase hidden repricing risk.
elements.rule_or_threshold: Flag any fee-change notice that omits explicit on-statement numbers
elements.trigger_condition: When statement says to go online for details
recommended_action: Treat as high-priority review event and request full written schedule.
source_trace: S09: 'you need to go online to see what those fees are'

elements.name: Backend family identification
elements.description: Many providers are resellers on a few major backends; statement pattern detection should leverage this.
elements.rule_or_threshold: Industry commonly maps to ~5-6 major backend styles
recommended_action: Maintain template library by backend pattern, not only brand name.
source_trace: S03: 'five or six big backends ... thousands of companies'

elements.name: Fee class: interchange/card-brand
elements.description: Card-brand/interchange assessments are foundational cost class and usually non-negotiable at wholesale.
recommended_action: Classify as baseline network cost for markup isolation.
source_trace: S02/S04 interchange discussion

elements.name: Fee class: processor basis-point markup
elements.description: Markup percent/basis-point component is primary negotiable processor charge.
recommended_action: Benchmark and negotiate based on bps and volume profile.
source_trace: S04/S07/S09 markup examples

elements.name: Fee class: per-transaction and authorization
elements.description: Per-item fees may be split into transaction and authorization components and must be combined.
recommended_action: Compute all-in per-item economics for low-ticket impact analysis.
source_trace: S09: worldpay shows transaction + authorization fee

elements.name: Fee class: monthly service/add-on
elements.description: Recurring subscription-like add-ons (analytics suites, platform add-ons) are separate from core processing economics.
recommended_action: Validate usage and cancel unused add-ons.
source_trace: S05: customer intelligence suite fee behavior

elements.name: Fee class: express/accelerated funding
elements.description: Faster-than-standard funding can carry an extra basis-point fee.
elements.rule_or_threshold: Standard mentioned as ~2 business days; faster funding may add 5 bps
elements.trigger_condition: When express funding line item appears
recommended_action: Confirm merchant actually requires accelerated funding before paying premium.
source_trace: S09: 'fund in two business days ... additional five basis points'

elements.name: Fee class: monthly minimum discount
elements.description: Monthly minimum imposes floor on processor markup paid regardless of actual earned markup.
elements.rule_or_threshold: Charge difference when actual markup < minimum
recommended_action: Model effective rate impact and negotiate removal or lower minimum.
source_trace: S09: '$25 minimum ... charged $17.64 difference'

elements.name: Fee class: PCI non-compliance
elements.description: PCI non-compliance fee indicates unresolved compliance process and potential security risk exposure.
recommended_action: Escalate PCI remediation immediately; treat as both cost and risk issue.
source_trace: S09/S10 non-compliance fee discussion

elements.name: Fee class: non-EMV penalty
elements.description: Processor-imposed non-EMV fee may stack fixed monthly charge and additional CNP markup.
elements.rule_or_threshold: Example trigger >10% non-EMV volume with $25 monthly + 1% extra markup
elements.trigger_condition: When non-EMV fee schedule is present
recommended_action: Classify as avoidable processor fee and evaluate alternatives.
source_trace: S05: non-EMV fee mechanics

elements.name: Fee class: risk fee
elements.description: Risk fee is an additional processor overlay used to offset portfolio risk and may not fit merchant profile.
recommended_action: Verify actual risk classification and challenge fee if profile is low-risk.
source_trace: S10: risk fee added in recent period

elements.name: Fee class: daily/monthly discount handling fee
elements.description: Some processors charge extra basis points for monthly discount handling or daily discount treatment.
elements.rule_or_threshold: Example observed: +5 bps
recommended_action: Treat as candidate avoidable fee and request removal.
source_trace: S04/S10: monthly vs daily discount costs and daily discount fee commentary

elements.name: Fee class: savings-share adjustment
elements.description: Commercial card savings adjustment can capture a percentage of interchange savings achieved.
elements.rule_or_threshold: Example observed: processor keeps 75% of savings
recommended_action: Evaluate whether processor should automate data pass-through without share fee.
source_trace: S07: 'commercial card interchange savings adjustment ... 75%'

elements.name: Over-market markup threshold
elements.description: Markup above a practical over-market threshold should be flagged as pricing risk.
elements.rule_or_threshold: >70 bps for most/new businesses considered over-marked in source guidance
recommended_action: Mark as over-market and queue repricing review.
source_trace: S04: 'anything over a 70 basis point markup ... overmarking data'

elements.name: Brand-specific markup inflation check
elements.description: Processor markup should not be arbitrarily higher on AmEx because of brand reputation.
elements.rule_or_threshold: AmEx can have higher interchange, not necessarily higher processor markup
recommended_action: Flag and request harmonized processor markup across brands.
source_trace: S04: 'no need to charge an additional fee ... on American Express'

elements.name: Debit-vs-credit markup inconsistency
elements.description: Different processor markup rates across debit and credit lines can indicate avoidable pricing distortion.
elements.trigger_condition: debit and credit markup materially differ without clear service justification
recommended_action: Flag for repricing and statement-level explanation.
source_trace: S07: 'we've seen these be different rates and there should not be different rates'

elements.name: Unused analytics suite fee
elements.description: Recurring analytics/product bundle fee is unnecessary if merchant cannot access or does not use value.
elements.rule_or_threshold: Observed around $55/month in source examples
recommended_action: Recommend cancellation and retroactive fee review where possible.
source_trace: S05/S10: customer intelligence suite often auto-starts after trial

elements.name: Auto-trial conversion detection
elements.description: New recurring products added via fine print and free trial expiry should be detected as stealth-fee risk.
elements.trigger_condition: Fee appears this month but absent prior month
recommended_action: Open processor inquiry immediately and classify as avoidable unless accepted in writing.
source_trace: S05: 'there one month that wasn't there prior month ... reach out'

elements.name: Non-EMV surcharge red flag
elements.description: Non-EMV penalties layered on top of existing interchange differences are treated as avoidable add-on markup.
elements.rule_or_threshold: Example: >10% non-EMV, +$25 monthly, +1% extra volume markup
recommended_action: Flag as high-priority optimization issue; consider processor replacement if persistent.
source_trace: S05/S10 non-EMV discussion

elements.name: Risk fee fit test
elements.description: Risk fee is potentially unnecessary when merchant profile has low chargeback and low advance-sale exposure.
recommended_action: Recommend risk-fee removal request or processor comparison.
source_trace: S10: 'not risky merchants ... paying fee to offset portfolio risk'

elements.name: Monthly minimum profitability trap
elements.description: Low advertised rates become irrelevant when monthly minimum consistently tops-up processor earnings.
elements.rule_or_threshold: Commonly assessed below ~$55,000 monthly volume
recommended_action: Model true all-in cost and remove floor in repricing.
source_trace: S09: monthly minimum explanation and sub-$55k volume note

elements.name: High per-item fee threshold
elements.description: Per-item economics become expensive when transaction+authorization total moves into high ranges.
elements.rule_or_threshold: Observed concern when all-in per-item > $0.40; target often low teens ($0.10-$0.15)
recommended_action: Classify as over-market per-item pricing and renegotiate.
source_trace: S09: 'over 40 cents ... should be in low teens 10 to 15 cents'

elements.name: Low-ticket merchant sensitivity
elements.description: For merchants with lower average tickets, per-item fees can dominate effective cost more than bps.
elements.rule_or_threshold: Highlighted around average transaction under $50
recommended_action: Prioritize per-item fee optimization before bps fine-tuning.
source_trace: S09: 'under a $50 average transaction size ... per item impacts more than basis points'

elements.name: Bundled pricing overpayment risk
elements.description: Bundled qualified/mid/non-qualified pricing often shifts benefit to processor and can materially overcharge.
elements.rule_or_threshold: Source guidance: 99/100 bundled programs overpay vs interchange-plus
recommended_action: Recommend migration path to interchange-plus analysis baseline.
source_trace: S08: '99 out of 100 times you're going to be overpaying'

elements.name: Extreme non-qualified rate signal
elements.description: Very high non-qualified/mid-qualified rates are immediate over-market indicators.
elements.rule_or_threshold: Source examples: non-qualified ~5%, mid-qualified ~4.09% flagged over-market
recommended_action: Escalate as urgent repricing or provider-change candidate.
source_trace: S08 bundled example rates

elements.name: Hidden markup-in-interchange detection
elements.description: Processor may hide markup in interchange section while presenting an artificially low explicit markup.
elements.rule_or_threshold: Source example: 10 bps shown + 30-40 bps embedded = true ~50 bps
elements.trigger_condition: Interchange totals inconsistent with expected card-brand schedules
recommended_action: Perform advanced audit and normalize all hidden markup components.
source_trace: S11 advanced hidden-fee explanation

elements.name: Fee jump notice from statement/legal text
elements.description: Explicit fee increases in notices (e.g., monthly fixed fee jump) should be tracked as repricing events.
elements.rule_or_threshold: Example in source: increase to $49.95 (+$20)
recommended_action: Log repricing event and reassess program economics immediately.
source_trace: S07 card point fee increase example

elements.name: Service-charge label mapping
elements.description: Line labels such as service charge can indicate processor markup embedded in mixed sections.
recommended_action: Map label taxonomy into standardized fee classes for consistent analytics.
source_trace: S07: 'where it says service charge that's going to give you idea ... fee from processor'

elements.name: Downgrade code detection
elements.description: Codes/labels like non-qualified or EIRF are downgrade signals indicating data submission gaps.
elements.trigger_condition: Labels contain non-qualified or EIRF-like terms
recommended_action: Tag as downgrade indicator and start data-capture remediation workflow.
source_trace: S07: 'non-qualified ... EIRF ... telling me customer is not passing correct data'

elements.name: Downgrade cost delta
elements.description: Missing required data can add measurable interchange cost uplift.
elements.rule_or_threshold: Source guidance: about 0.3%-0.4% more
recommended_action: Quantify downgrade penalty and include in savings opportunity output.
source_trace: S07/S12: 'paying about .3 to .4% more'

elements.name: Card-present risk class awareness
elements.description: Networks treat card-present as lower risk and price it accordingly.
recommended_action: Use entry-mode mix as an explanatory feature in fee analysis.
source_trace: S12: 'card present transaction as least risky transaction'

elements.name: Card-not-present baseline premium
elements.description: Card-not-present transactions typically incur higher base interchange than card-present.
elements.rule_or_threshold: Source guidance around 0.3%-0.4% differential
recommended_action: Separate CNP base premium from processor-added penalties in reporting.
source_trace: S05/S12: CNP described as ~0.3-0.4% more within interchange

elements.name: CNP required data field completeness
elements.description: CNP optimization requires collecting and transmitting mandatory verification/order context fields.
elements.rule_or_threshold: PO number required for business card optimization scenarios
recommended_action: Validate software prompts and gateway mappings for all required CNP fields.
source_trace: S12: billing street, zip, PO number, CVV requirements

elements.name: Terminal prompt coverage
elements.description: If POS/terminal does not prompt for required data, all affected transactions may downgrade.
elements.trigger_condition: Prompt config missing required CNP fields
recommended_action: Escalate software configuration issue and remediate prompt workflow.
source_trace: S12: 'if terminal is not prompting ... you're going to be downgrading all those transactions'

elements.name: Level-3 eligibility detection
elements.description: B2B/B2G commercial transactions may qualify for interchange incentives with level-3 submission.
recommended_action: Enable level-3 workflow review and card-type qualification logic.
source_trace: S12: 'B2B ... certain business cards qualify for incentive rates called level three data'

elements.name: Level-3 data field checklist
elements.description: Level-3 optimization depends on transaction detail fields beyond standard auth fields.
recommended_action: Validate ERP/POS mapping of all level-3 fields to processor feed.
source_trace: S12: invoice number, product code, quantity, item description, commodity code

elements.name: Level-3 savings potential
elements.description: Proper level-3 submission can materially reduce interchange in qualifying flows.
elements.rule_or_threshold: Source states reductions can exceed 1% in some cases
recommended_action: Surface level-3 enablement as high-impact optimization opportunity.
source_trace: S12: 'lower those interchange rates by ... over a percent'

elements.name: PCI baseline obligations
elements.description: PCI process includes annual SAQ and vulnerability scans; analysis must track compliance status and cycle.
elements.rule_or_threshold: Annual recertification implied
recommended_action: Track due dates and non-compliance exposure as separate control panel metric.
source_trace: S09/S13: annual self-assessment questionnaire and vulnerability scans

elements.name: PCI industry under-compliance context
elements.description: Low compliance prevalence should increase urgency of proactive remediation guidance.
elements.rule_or_threshold: Source Q&A: only ~33% PCI compliant
recommended_action: Escalate severity and provide remediation checklist, not only fee warning.
source_trace: S13: 'only about 33% of businesses are PCI compliant today'

elements.name: Breach consequence differential
elements.description: Compliance status affects severity of post-breach financial penalties and response outcomes.
recommended_action: Include security-risk narrative in addition to cost narrative for non-compliance findings.
source_trace: S10/S13: card brands 'take it a lot easier' when compliant

elements.name: Card-present optimization lever
elements.description: Whenever operationally possible, converting CNP volume to card-present lowers cost.
elements.rule_or_threshold: Source guidance indicates ~0.3%-0.4% lower for card-present
recommended_action: Recommend channel/process changes that increase card-present share.
source_trace: S12: 'anytime you can take a card in person you're going to want to do that'

elements.name: Processor-assisted data automation lever
elements.description: Processor should help automate required data pass-through (especially level-3) rather than charging avoidable penalties.
elements.trigger_condition: When downgrade or savings-share fees appear
recommended_action: Prioritize processors/gateways with automation for required card data submission.
source_trace: S12: processor can help automate level-3 data process

elements.name: Interchange-plus migration lever
elements.description: Interchange-plus/cost-plus pricing generally yields better transparency and cost control than bundled pricing.
recommended_action: Recommend conversion analysis to interchange-plus model.
source_trace: S08: cost-plus described as more cost effective in long run

elements.name: Unnecessary fee cleanup lever
elements.description: Removing avoidable add-on fees is a first-order savings lever independent of interchange movement.
recommended_action: Prioritize removal of non-EMV, risk, unused suite, daily discount, and similar add-ons.
source_trace: S10: explicit list of unnecessary fee types

elements.name: Per-item repricing lever
elements.description: All-in per-item fees should be benchmarked and repriced, especially for low-ticket merchants.
elements.rule_or_threshold: Practical target range cited as low teens in source
recommended_action: Negotiate lower per-item terms and remove duplicate authorization add-ons.
source_trace: S09: low-teens benchmark and low-ticket impact

elements.name: Monthly minimum elimination lever
elements.description: Removing monthly minimum floors prevents hidden rate inflation for lower-volume merchants.
recommended_action: Negotiate elimination/reduction or switch to plan without minimum floor.
source_trace: S09 monthly minimum behavior

elements.name: Surcharge model definition
elements.description: Surcharging passes credit card fee directly to customer as separate add-on.
recommended_action: Verify policy implementation matches surcharge definition and rulebook.
source_trace: S13 surcharge definition

elements.name: Cash discount model definition
elements.description: Cash discount builds card cost into posted price and offers discount for cash payment.
recommended_action: Verify signage and receipt logic align with cash-discount model.
source_trace: S13 cash discount definition

elements.name: Surcharge cap enforcement
elements.description: Surcharge percentages must enforce current card-brand cap.
elements.rule_or_threshold: Max surcharge = 3%
recommended_action: Block configuration and enforce max-3% compliance.
source_trace: S13: 'max you can now surcharge is 3%'

elements.name: Debit surcharge prohibition
elements.description: Debit transactions must be excluded from surcharge application.
elements.rule_or_threshold: Never surcharge debit
recommended_action: Mark as compliance failure and correct POS rules immediately.
source_trace: S13/S14: 'not able to surcharge debit cards'

elements.name: Uniform surcharge application rule
elements.description: If surcharging is used, it must apply to all eligible credit transactions, not selected amounts/customers.
elements.rule_or_threshold: Surcharge one => surcharge all eligible credit transactions
elements.trigger_condition: Policy applies surcharge only above thresholds or to selected customers
recommended_action: Flag non-compliant selective surcharge behavior and enforce uniform rule.
source_trace: S14: 'if you're going to surcharge one you've got to surcharge all'

elements.name: Debit identification control
elements.description: Debit detection should be automated via BIN/terminal logic when possible; fallback is manual identification.
recommended_action: Enable automated debit detection; provide manual controls only as backup.
source_trace: S14: BIN-first-six detection or manual 'debit' card label

elements.name: Jurisdictional legality capture
elements.description: Tool should store location legality context for surcharge/cash discount policy outputs.
elements.rule_or_threshold: Source explicitly confirms both legal in Wisconsin
recommended_action: Attach state-level legality note to policy recommendation output.
source_trace: S14: surcharge and cash discount legal in Wisconsin

elements.name: Review cadence policy
elements.description: Merchant statements should be re-evaluated regularly due routine repricing behavior in market.
elements.rule_or_threshold: Re-evaluate at least every 6-12 months
recommended_action: Set recurring review task and alert when cadence breached.
source_trace: S14: pricing changes every 6-12 months

elements.name: Month-over-month fee drift watch
elements.description: Any newly introduced fee line item should trigger immediate validation workflow.
recommended_action: Require processor explanation and merchant acceptance status check.
source_trace: S05/S07: watch for new fees and fine-print repricing

elements.name: Pricing strategy durability check
elements.description: Savings claims must be evaluated for durability, not only initial quote-month benefit.
recommended_action: Assess repricing policy and expected savings half-life in recommendation output.
source_trace: S14: 'if they're promising savings how long will those savings be there'

elements.name: Effective-rate contextual benchmark
elements.description: Global effective-rate benchmark can be shown with cautionary context that actual target depends on mix.
elements.rule_or_threshold: Source high-level range: ~2.5%-3.5% (context-dependent)
recommended_action: Display benchmark as directional, not absolute pass/fail threshold.
source_trace: S14: good rate question answered with 'depends' and 2.5%-3.5% range

elements.name: Volume-linked markup expectation
elements.description: Markup should generally decrease as merchant volume grows; upward trend is adverse signal.
elements.rule_or_threshold: Higher-volume merchants expected to have lower markup than startups
recommended_action: Trigger repricing negotiation recommendation.
source_trace: S14: 'as volume grows your markup should go down'

elements.name: Decision trigger: persistent over-market pricing
elements.description: Multiple over-market fee indicators should escalate from optimization advice to processor-change recommendation.
elements.rule_or_threshold: Escalate when high-severity fee issues persist across cycles
elements.trigger_condition: Repeated over-market or avoidable fees unresolved
recommended_action: Recommend evaluating alternate processor proposals with representative-month analysis.
source_trace: S10/S14 repeated guidance: may be time to look for different processor

elements.name: Decision trigger: non-compliance unmanaged
elements.description: If processor does not actively help with PCI compliance while charging non-compliance fees, escalation is warranted.
recommended_action: Recommend processor/service escalation or replacement consideration.
source_trace: S10: if not getting help and being billed non-compliance fee, consider change

elements.name: Decision trigger: CNP business penalized by non-EMV overlay
elements.description: Card-not-present merchants paying layered non-EMV penalties should be marked high-priority for repricing/provider review.
elements.trigger_condition: business_model is CNP-heavy and non-EMV overlays present
recommended_action: Recommend immediate alternative provider comparison.
source_trace: S05/S10 CNP merchants highlighted as hardest hit by non-EMV add-on

elements.name: Decision trigger: hidden or opaque repricing
elements.description: Opaque notices (fine print or online-only change references) increase trust and predictability risk.
recommended_action: Escalate to vendor governance and consider transparent-pricing alternatives.
source_trace: S07/S09/S14: repeated warnings around hidden increases

elements.name: Decision trigger: manual debit handling burden
elements.description: If debit identification for surcharge compliance is mostly manual, operational error risk may outweigh surcharge benefit.
elements.trigger_condition: surcharge enabled and debit detection not automated
recommended_action: Recommend automation upgrade or policy simplification before expansion.
source_trace: S14: manual staff detection called out as imperfect system

elements.name: Decision trigger: unexplained fee growth
elements.description: Any sustained fee growth without corresponding service/value evidence should trigger strategic review.
recommended_action: Initiate formal fee audit and competitive bid process.
source_trace: S05/S07/S14: monitor increases and reassess regularly

source_sections.section_title: Webinar goals and merchant pain points
source_sections.summary: Read statements, identify real vs unnecessary fees, and reduce costs.
Two fee buckets, interchange complexity, fixed wholesale concept, net effective rate.
Use trend graph to avoid cherry-picked high-sales month and understand backend patterning.
Row-level interchange fields, markup interpretation, basis-point notation, over-market threshold examples.

source_sections.section_title: Ancillary fee examples and emerging overlays
source_sections.summary: Non-EMV penalties, customer suite auto-billing, fee appearance checks.
Top/bottom number split where processor and interchange are shown together.

source_sections.section_title: Fine print, downgrade markers, and savings adjustment
source_sections.summary: Fee-change notices, service-charge mapping, EIRF/non-qualified clues, and 75% savings share example.
Qualified/mid/non-qualified buckets and frequent overpayment risk.
Express funding, monthly minimum, PCI non-compliance, stacked per-item auth fees, online-only notices.
Non-EMV, risk fee, customer suite, daily discount, PCI non-compliance, and hidden markup patterns.
Markup can be concealed inside interchange while headline markup appears low.
Card-present mix, CNP required fields, terminal prompts, level-3 capture and savings.
Definitions, surcharge max cap, debit exclusion, and compliance posture.
Rate range context, review cadence, legality in Wisconsin, surcharge-all rule, and debit detection methods.

coverage_checks.notes: All analysis-relevant sections from model explanation, statement walk-through, fee red flags, optimization tactics, and Q&A rules mapped.
Includes major and trivial checks: fine print, new monthly fee appearance, debit identification, review cadence, and surcharge edge constraints.

Numeric thresholds preserved from source: 60-80%, 20-40%, >70 bps, >10% non-EMV trigger, $25 monthly minimum examples, 75% savings share, 3% surcharge cap, 2.5-3.5% benchmark context, 6-12 month review cadence, 10-15 cent per-item target context.
Processor-specific examples normalized into universal elements while retaining original evidence in source_trace references.
Every element includes source_trace with section references and quoted anchors.
No unresolved contradictions remain after dedup; surcharge/cash-discount and compliance rules are internally coherent.

dedup_notes.rationale: Metric definition and formula describe the same core KPI and were unified.

merged_from: non-EMV extra percentage on CNP volume
dedup_notes.rationale: Both are parts of one composite non-EMV penalty structure.
All evaluate the same all-in per-item economics and were merged into one thresholded element.
Different descriptor names point to one downgrade-signal concept.

merged_from: PO number requirement for business cards
dedup_notes.rationale: All are mandatory/qualifying CNP data-capture elements.
Unified to a single cost-cleanup lever for implementation clarity.

merged_from: check if fee new this month
dedup_notes.rationale: Both describe month-over-month drift detection with immediate investigation trigger.
